title: ApprovalRequest
description: A request for human approval, created when the policy engine returns `RequireApproval` or `RequireProof`.

action_id.description: The action that triggered this approval.

action_request.description: The full action request, preserved for asynchronous dispatch after approval.

action_summary.description: Summary of the proposed action for display in the portal.

approval_id.description: Unique identifier for this approval request.

policy_id.description: ID of the policy rule that triggered this requirement.

requested_at.description: Timestamp when the approval request was created.

ActionDescriptor.description:
Describes a concrete action that an agent or automation wants to execute.

This replaces the old raw tool-name dispatch. Every action is now classified by name, category, operation kind, optional monetary amount, and risk-tagging hints.

amount.description: Monetary value involved, if any (used for threshold-based policy).

arguments.description: Raw arguments to pass to the executor.

resource.description: Optional resource identifier (e.g. order ID, calendar ID).

tags.description: Free-form risk/classification tags, e.g. ["mutation", "external_api", "payout_change"]. Policy rules can match on these.

ActionRequest.description:
The canonical action request that flows through the entire gateway.

Created when any proposer (ssi_agent, webhook, WhatsApp bridge, PicoClaw) submits an action for governance.

action.description: What is being requested — the action descriptor.

action_id.description: Unique identifier for this action attempt.

actor.description: Identity context: who is asking and under what authority.

source.description: Where the action originated (ssi_agent, whatsapp, webhook, etc.).

tenant_id.description: Tenant that owns the resources being acted upon.

ActionSummary.description: Human-readable summary of an action, used in approval cards.

operation.description: What kind of operation (read, create, update, delete, transfer).

ActorContext.description:
Who is performing the action and under what authority.

Derived from the session JWT issued by `ssi_vault`.

auth_level.description: Current authentication level of the actor.

owner_did.description: DID of the entity that owns the tenant / agent box (from JWT `iss`).

requester_did.description: DID of the entity that initiated the action (from JWT `sub`). May be the same as `owner_did` for first-party requests.

user_did.description: Optional end-user DID when the action is on behalf of a customer.

ApprovalTier.description:
The tier of approval required for a governed action.

Higher tiers provide stronger assurance but more friction. The policy engine maps actions to tiers based on risk attributes.

oneOf.description: Tier 0: auto-allow. No human intervention needed.
Tier 1: simple portal click. Owner sees the action and approves.

Tier 3: the USER must present a verifiable credential via their wallet (OpenID4VP). This is the strongest assurance level.

**Corrected flow**: the user/holder presents proof. The portal renders the QR code; the gateway/Host verifies.

AuthLevel.description:
Authentication strength of the current session.

Used by the policy engine to decide whether step-up is needed.

oneOf.description: Basic session token — the default after Host login.

amount_minor.description: Amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents for EUR).

OperationKind.description: The kind of operation an action performs.

name.description: Optional human-readable name for the source instance.
